message:
fix(search/noise,bd-q6xf9): don't classify hits with empty content as noise

hit_is_noise() combined two text-based signals:

    is_search_noise_text(content, query)
        || (!content.is_empty() && is_tool_invocation_noise(content))

but is_search_noise_text() is only defined as "noise" when it finds
positive evidence of noise *in the text*. On an empty string, the
guard was flipping the classifier into "yes, noise" for every hit.
When a caller asks for a projection that omits both `content` and
`snippet` (e.g. `cass search --fields minimal` or `--fields summary`),
hit_content_for_noise_check() returns "", so every real match was
being silently dropped. Reported via reality-check bead q6xf9:
`cass search --fields minimal` returned zero hits on demo data even
when matches existed.

The noise classifier can't make a correctness-preserving decision
with nothing to inspect, so short-circuit empty content to "not
noise" and let the hit through; the caller's requested field
projection will apply downstream.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
